request.question: What nationality is the only athlete who has won both Senior All-Ireland Championship medal and this contest in 2005 in Melbourne attended by 91,898 spectators.

title: Tadhg Kennelly
Kerry GAA honours
Emer Dillon
David Curtin
Rachel Moloney
Mark Davoren
Ireland National League (rugby league)
2005 AFL Grand Final
Jenny Duffy
Brian Farrell (Gaelic footballer)
sentences: Tadhg Kennelly (born 1 July 1981) is an Irish sportsperson known for his top level careers in both Gaelic football and Australian rules football.
He is the only holder of both an AFL Premiership medallion and a Senior All-Ireland Championship medal, the highest possible achievement in both sports.
He has also represented Ireland in the International Rules Series.
Kerry are by far the most successful team in the history of Gaelic football.
They have won 37 All-Ireland Senior Football Championship and have appeared in countless other All-Ireland Football Finals.
Kerry footballers have been garlanded with countless awards and hold numerous individual records in the sport.

Pat Spillane received nine All Stars during a glittering career, a feat matched by no other Gaelic footballer, while Tadhg Kennelly is the only holder of both an AFL Premiership medallion and a Senior All-Ireland Championship medal, the highest possible achievement in the sports of Australian rules football and Gaelic football.
Here are Kerry's honours.
Emer Dillon born 1984 in Cork is a camogie player and a marketing executive, winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2002, 2005, 2006, and 2009 and a camogie All Star in 2005.
Emer was selected as player of the match in the 2005 All-Ireland Senior final.
She is also a prominent hockey player and has represented both Munster and Ireland.
She holds a Senior county championship medal with Carrigdhoun and also Junior and under-age championship honours with Ballygarvan.
She has won All-Ireland Senior, Intermediate and Minor medals as well as inter-provincial honours but did not participate in the 2008 championship due to study.
David Curtin (born 13 September 1980) is an inter-county hurling player who plays for Dublin.
He plays his club hurling for Dublin side Ballyboden St. Enda's.
In 2005, he will be remembered for his astounding performance against Laois which confirmed Dublins All-Ireland status for the 2006 championship.
Curtin finished the game with an impressive scoreline of 1-7.
In the 2006 All-Ireland championship, this feat was repeated when Dublin swept aside Westmeath to once retain their Liam McCarthy Cup status.
He finished the 2006 championship with a scoreline of 0-33, a scoreline which left him 5th of the top scorer list despite Dublins lack of progress in the All-Ireland championship.
Curtin was the Dublin hurling captain for 2005.
David was named on the 2006 Dublin Bus/Evening Herald Blue Star hurling XV at Centre Forward.
Curtin won his first Dublin Senior Hurling Championship medal with his club Ballyboden St Endas in October 2007.
Since then Boden have won 4 titles in a row.
The 2007 Dublin championship win qualified Ballyboden to play in their first ever Leinster Senior Club Hurling Championship game against Oulart the Ballagh GAA of Wexford.
Boden won the game by 1-17 to 0-15 with Curtin scoring a total of 0-04.
Rachel Moloney born 1982 in Cork is a camogie player and a marketing executive, winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2008, and 2009 and camogie All Star awards in 2005, 2008 and 2009.
She holds Ashbourne Cup medals with U.C.C. as well as Minor, Junior, Intermediate and Senior All-Ireland championship honours with Cork.
Has won inter-provincial honours with Munster and is the side's free-taker and top scorer.
Rachel is coach to her club's Under-14 team.
Mark Davoren is an Irish Gaelic footballer for Dublin and Kilmacud Crokes.
He made his debut for Dublin against Kerry in the National Football League (Ireland) in 2009 scoring a total of 1-03 in the game which finished as a draw in Parnell Park.
He won an All-Ireland Senior Club Football Championship medal with Kilmacud Crokes on St Patricks Day 2009 in Croke Park against Crosmaglen Rangers.
He scored a goal in the game.
Davoren won a Leinster Senior Club Football Championship medal with Crokes against Rhode of Offalyin 2008 at Parnell Park, he scored 1-00 in the final.
He won the Dublin Senior Football Championship in 2008 by beating St Oliver Plunketts/Eoghan Ruadh in Parnell Park scoring a goal in the process.
He won an AFL Dublin Division One title with Kilmacud Crokes in 2008, although he did not make an appearance in the league final.
The All-Ireland Rugby League Championship (now the McGettigans All-Ireland League for sponsorship reasons) was first played for in 1997 under the title All-Ireland Challenge Cup.
Since then it has also been known as the All-Ireland League, Elite League and the All-Ireland Championship.
The 2005 AFL Grand Final was an Australian rules football game contested between the Sydney Swans and West Coast Eagles at the Melbourne Cricket Ground on 24 September 2005.
It was the 109th annual Grand Final of the Australian Football League (formerly the Victorian Football League), staged to determine the premiers for the 2005 AFL season.
The match, attended by 91,898 spectators, was won by Sydney by a margin of four points, marking the club's fourth Premiership and their first since 1933.
Jennifer "Jenny" Duffy (born 1983 in Cork) is a camogie player, footballer and camogie development officer, winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2009.
A former student at Cork IT, Jenny is the Camogie Regional Development officer in South Leinster.
She is the holder of All-Ireland championship and league medals with CIT as well as Senior and Junior All-Ireland championship medals and National League.
She won a Senior county championship with her club and captained CIT to the Ashbourne Shield title in 2006.
Brian Farrell is a Gaelic footballer from Nobber, County Meath, Ireland.
He plays with the Meath county team and his local club Nobber.

He first came to national attention when he was part of the Meath team that made it to the 2002 All-Ireland Minor Football Championship final but lost out to Derry having already been beaten by Longford in the Leinster Championship final.
The following year, he was part of the Meath side that won the Leinster and All-Ireland Junior Football Championships.
At club level, he has won County and Leinster Junior medals in 2002 and an All-Ireland Junior Club Football Championship medal in 2003.
In 2010, Farrell won a Meath Intermediate Football Championship medal.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- If summary_1 already includes explicit profession/role/descriptor tokens for an anchor (for example, 'singer', 'director', 'country music artist'), omit those redundant descriptor tokens and emit a compact anchor-only query consisting of the core visible anchors (names, titles, groups). Retain relation tokens only when the question explicitly requests them or when disambiguation rules demand them. Do not remove descriptors when the question explicitly asks for role/occupation evidence or when a short-title disambiguation rule requires adding an entertainment disambiguator.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity (or otherwise names candidate/bridge entities), treat the context claim as the summary anchor and perform explicit identity-alignment checks before using passage facts. For each entity explicitly named in `context` as a candidate or bridge, do the following: if at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append a single brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that linking fact and references the corroborating passage and also include one short clause for that context-named entity giving the passage-supported relation value (e.g., "Name: [relation value]."); if no passage provides the relation value for that context-named entity, append a short clause stating that no relation value is provided in the evidence (e.g., "Name: no [relation] provided in evidence."). Do not create such clauses for entities that are only mentioned in `passages` and are not named in `context`. Do not append corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep these additions short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.
- Vocal-role single-token normalization (narrow scope): If summary_2 (alone or composed with summary_1 where identity links are explicit) explicitly contains vocal-role evidence — i.e., one or more verbalized vocal-role phrases that clearly denote a singing performer (examples: "lead singer", "lead vocals", "vocalist" as presented in the summaries) — then you may prefer to emit the concise single-token common noun for a person who sings (e.g., "singer") only when that single-token common noun itself appears verbatim in the summaries or when the vocal-role phrases in the summaries unambiguously denote the same person-role. If those explicit lexical licenses are not present, retain the literal role phrasing as given in summary_2 and do not normalize modifiers or drop qualifiers.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What nationality is the only athlete who has won both Senior All-Ireland Championship medal and this contest in 2005 in Melbourne attended by 91,898 spectators.
hop1_docs: 2005 AFL Grand Final | The 2005 AFL Grand Final was an Australian rules football game contested between the Sydney Swans and West Coast Eagles at the Melbourne Cricket Ground on 24 September 2005.  It was the 109th annual Grand Final of the Australian Football League (formerly the Victorian Football League), staged to determine the premiers for the 2005 AFL season.  The match, attended by 91,898 spectators, was won by Sydney by a margin of four points, marking the club's fourth Premiership and their first since 1933.

Emer Dillon | Emer Dillon born 1984 in Cork is a camogie player and a marketing executive, winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2002, 2005, 2006, and 2009 and a camogie All Star in 2005.  Emer was selected as player of the match in the 2005 All-Ireland Senior final.  She is also a prominent hockey player and has represented both Munster and Ireland.  She holds a Senior county championship medal with Carrigdhoun and also Junior and under-age championship honours with Ballygarvan.  She has won All-Ireland Senior, Intermediate and Minor medals as well as inter-provincial honours but did not participate in the 2008 championship due to study.

Cookstown Fr. Rock's GAC | Cookstown Father Rocks Gaelic Athletic Club is a Gaelic Athletic Association club based in Cookstown in County Tyrone, Northern Ireland.  In recent years Owen Mulligan and Raymond Mulgrew have both won All-Ireland Senior Football Championship medals with the Tyrone senior team.  The club also had five players who won All-Ireland Minor Football Championship medals in the same day in 2004.

2005 All-Ireland Senior Camogie Championship | The 2005 All-Ireland Senior Camogie Championship—known as the Foras na Gaeilge All-Ireland Senior Camogie Championship for sponsorship reasons—was the high point of the 2005 season in the sport of camogie.  The championship was won for the 21st time by Cork who defeated Tipperary by a four-point margin in the final and became part of the legendary “rebel treble” of 2005 when Cork won the senior hurling, camogie and ladies’ football titles.  The attendance was 14,350.

Ollie Murphy | Ollie Murphy is a Meath Footballer.  He plays club football for Carnaross.  He came to national prominence in 1999 when he was one of Meath's best player's as they won the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship that year, scoring the decisive goal in that year's All Ireland final.  This was his second All Ireland senior medal as he was a substitute when Meath won the 1996 All-Ireland Senior Football Championship.  He is also the holder of 3 Leinster Senior Football Championship medals.  Ollie has won 2 GAA All Stars Awards.  Murphys best season was the 2001 championship where he put in a number of superb display's as Meath reached another All Ireland final.  In that final he had to leave the field of play with a hand injury.  In recent years his form has been not up to what it once was and he has failed to warrant a starting position.  Ollie made his senior inter-county debut playing for London in the 1994 Connacht Championship against Galway.  It was his one and only appearance for the Exiles.  Murphy's Benny father was also an inter-county player, he lined out for his native Fermanagh

Aoife Murray | Aoife Murray (born 1984 in Dunmanway) is a Camogie player and quantity surveyor based in Cork, winner of All Ireland medals in 2002, 2005, 2006, 2008, 2009, 2014 and 2015.  All Star awards in 2004, 2008, 2009, 2012, 2014 and 2015.  Minor, Junior, Intermediate as well as her Senior All-Ireland medals and Munster championship honours in all grades.  Won three county Senior championship medals with Cloughduv and was captain in 2005 for their third county title.  Sister of former Cork Senior hurler, Kevin Murray, who won an All- Ireland medal in 1999.  A series of impressive saves in the 2009 All Ireland final won for her the "player of the match” award from RTÉ.

1989 All-Ireland Senior Camogie Championship | The 1989 All-Ireland Senior Camogie Championship was won by Killkenny who defeated Cork by an eight-point margin in the final.  The match drew an attendance of 3,024 and marked Angela Downey’s ninth All Ireland medal.
response.summary_1:
- Passage fact: the "2005 AFL Grand Final" was played "on September 24, 2005" and "the match, attended by 91,898 spectators, was won by Sydney" (passage 1).  
- Other passages list athletes who have won Senior All‑Ireland medals (examples: "Emer Dillon ... winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2002, 2005, 2006, and 2009" (passage 2); "Aoife Murray ... winner of All Ireland medals in 2002, 2005, 2006, 2008, 2009, 2014 and 2015" (passage 6); "Owen Mulligan ... have both won All-Ireland Senior Football Championship medals with the Tyrone senior team" (passage 3); "Ollie Murphy ... Meath ... won the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship" (passage 5)).  
- Missing bridge: none of the provided passages link any named All‑Ireland Senior medalist to being a member/winner of the Sydney team that won the 2005 AFL Grand Final. The next hop must identify which player (a member of the 2005 Sydney Swans premiership team) also holds a Senior All‑Ireland Championship medal, and then retrieve that player's nationality.  
- Retrieval anchors/queries for next hop: search for "2005 Sydney Swans premiership team member All‑Ireland Senior medal", "player who won 2005 AFL Grand Final and an All‑Ireland Senior Championship", or look up the 2005 Sydney Swans list of players and check which of them has an All‑Ireland Senior medal (to then get that player's nationality).
response.hop2_query: 2005 Sydney Swans premiership team All-Ireland Senior medal nationality
hop2_docs: Stephen O'Neill | Stephen O'Neill (born 19 November 1980) is an Irish Gaelic footballer from Aughabrack, Northern Ireland, who plays for the Tyrone senior football team.  He won three All-Ireland Senior Football Championship medals, two Under 21 medals, and a Minor medal.  He was the 2005 All Stars Footballer of the Year, and won All Stars Awards in 2001, 2005 and 2009.

Catherine Doherty (camogie) | Catherine Doherty is a camogie player and a purchaser, who played in the 2009 All Ireland camogie final.  She played cornerforward on her first All Ireland final appearance in 2001.  and her brother, Noel, who won an All-Ireland Intermediate medal in 2008.  Another brother, Niall, has also donned the county colours in various grades while an uncle, Joe Doherty, played Senior with Kilkenny in 1965.  Catherine won a Purcell Cup medal with Carlow I.T. and was picked on the combined Purcell team in 2005.  She played camogie/shinty for Ireland in Centenary Year and represented Leinster in the Gael Linn in 2000, 2002, 2005 and 2009.  Has won a National League and Leinster Junior and Senior titles with Kilkenny, as well as Junior and Intermediate medals with the club.  Her senior debut was in 2000.

Orla Cotter | Orla Cotter (born 1988 in Cork) is a Camogie player and student, winner of All-Ireland Camogie medals in 2006, 2008, 2009, 2014 and 2015.  and a Camogie All Star award in 2008, 2014, 2015 and 2016.  Captained the Cork Minor team in 2006, having won an All-Ireland Under-16 medal three years earlier.  She has won two Senior All- Ireland medals and made history in 2006 when she became the first player to collect Senior and Senior 'B' honours in the same year.  Orla holds titles with her club from Under-14 to Junior as well as All-Ireland Junior Colleges honours and Senior and Junior National League medals.  Her sister, Fiona, was on the Cork Under-16 team in 2008.

Una O'Donoghue | Una O'Donoghue born 1981 in Lissarda, County Cork, Ireland is a camogie player, winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2002, when she was team captain, 2005, 2006, 2008 and 2009.  She led her club to their first county Senior championship title in 2001.  She is the holder of Ashbourne Cup and league honours with UCC.  as well as National League medals with Cork.  She has won three county Senior medals in total with her club.

Imelda Kennedy | Imelda Kennedy is a camogie player, winner of an All-Star award in 2006.  She was a member of the Kilkenny team that won the All-Ireland Senior Camogie Championship in 1994, and won All Ireland Club Championship medals with St Lachtain’s, Freshford in 2004, 2005 and 2006 and All Ireland titles with Kilkenny at minor level, as well as two Leinster junior medals, two All-Ireland club medals, four Leinster Club medals and eight county championships with her club.

Joanne O'Callaghan | Joanne O'Callaghan born 1983 in Cork is a camogie player and pharmacy representative, winner of All Ireland medals in 2002, 2005, 2006, 2008 , 2009 and 2014.  She captained Cork to the All-Ireland title in 2006 and is in her 13th season on the Senior team.  She also has Minor and Intermediate All-Ireland championship medals to her credit as well as Ashbourne Cup and Gael Linn honours.  She holds three county Senior championship medals with her club, Cloughduv.

Lynda O'Connell | Lynda O'Connell (born 1981 in Cork) is a camogie player and an accountant, winner of All Ireland camogie medals in 2002 and 2005 and 2008 and 2009.  She captained Cork to the National League Junior title in 2005 and is a holder of two Ashbourne Cup medals with UCC as well as Senior, Intermediate and Junior All-Ireland medals.  Also holds Munster championship titles with Cork as well as a Senior county championship with her club and inter-provincial honours with Munster.
response.summary_2: The 2005 AFL Grand Final (24 Sep 2005), attended by 91,898 spectators, was won by the Sydney Swans. None of the supplied passages link any All‑Ireland Senior medalist to being a member/winner of Sydney’s 2005 team — Emer Dillon: no relation provided in evidence; Aoife Murray: no relation provided in evidence; Owen Mulligan: no relation provided in evidence; Ollie Murphy: no relation provided in evidence.
